heads.text: Building in a domain I did not understand yet

nodes.text: Speed was real. Trust was not automatic. The project needed evidence before production con
VELOCITY 308 commits
LEARNING WINDOW 11 weeks
BOUNDARY 0 live trades before safety was credible
The process became more important than the prompt.

heads.text: The repo moved fast. The domain had to be earned.

nodes.text: WHAT FOOLS YOU Looks good A polished dashboard, profitable backtest, and confident explana
WHAT CAN BE HIDING Still wrong Stale data, mismatched assumptions, or missing gates can tu
The question is not "does it look right?" It is: can we prove it?

heads.text: In some domains, a wrong answer looks convincing

nodes.text: 1 Human screens Trading Day Discovery Backtest Lab Pipeline 2 Rules engine Strategies, sch
1 Human screens Trading Day Discovery Backtest Lab Pipeline
2 Rules engine Strategies, schedules, execution logic
3 Safety layer Risk checks, limits, approvals, no-trade reasons
4 Evidence layer Market data, backtests, decision history
5 Real-world boundary Broker integration and actual order execution
AI helped build parts of this, but it does not get to bypass the checks.

heads.text: A system for ideas, checks, risk, decisions, and control.
Risk checks, limits, approvals, no-trade reasons
Broker integration and actual order execution

heads.text: The three questions under every screen
What kind of market are we in?
What are we allowed to do?
Why did it say yes or no?

nodes.text: THE USEFUL LOOP 01 Ask 02 Build 03 Test 04 Review 05 Learn
01 Ask
02 Build
03 Test
04 Review
05 Learn
Good at Explaining code, drafting APIs, writing tests, and comparing options.
Dangerous when I skipped review and treated fluent confidence as correctness.

heads.text: AI did not magically know trading
It made the learning loop faster.

nodes.text: BEFORE Mismatch Research could look fine while live sizing would behave differently.
AFTER Truthfulness Backtests had to match production assumptions before they could guide d

heads.text: The moment "looks good" was not good enough
The backtest and the live system were sizing trades differently.

nodes.text: EARLY Big prompt 1 Ask broadly The task sounded clear until the diff arrived. 2 Big diff T
EARLY Big prompt 1 Ask broadly The task sounded clear until the diff arrived. 2 Big diff T
1 Ask broadly The task sounded clear until the diff arrived.
2 Big diff Too much changed at once.
3 Review painfully Correctness was hard to see.
LATER Smaller loop 1 Spec + plan Write the domain and acceptance criteria first. 2 Isolate
1 Spec + plan Write the domain and acceptance criteria first.
2 Isolate Keep work small enough to understand.
3 Test + review Verify, then merge.

Split the work Turn broad ideas into task packs and acceptance checks.
Ask for proof Tests, audits, and logs beat fluent explanations.
Keep humans in control Risky actions need approval, not automation theater.

nodes.text: The model is not the expert. It is a fast helper.
The workflow builds expertise. Ask better. Check more. Own the judgment.

heads.text: AI helped me move faster. Checks helped me move safely.
The model is not the expert.
